description: Complete game pass — fix all broken wiring, add missing features, build real UI, add audio foundation, and implement game feel. Transform the scaffold into a playable game.

userStories.title: Fix pause system — actually pause all gameplay systems
userStories.description: As a player, I want the game to truly pause when I press Escape so that enemies stop attacking and time stops.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a not_paused() run condition function that reads PauseState resource
Guard player_movement, player_attack, enemy_ai, enemy_spawn, enemy_despawn, gather_resources, farming growth, and weather systems with .run_if(not_paused)
DayNightCycle already has .paused field — ensure it is checked in the daynight update system
Combat damage, projectile movement, and NPC systems also gated by not_paused
HUD rendering systems are NOT paused (menu must still render)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create pub fn not_paused(pause: Res<PauseState>) -> bool { !pause.paused } in hud.rs and export it. Add .run_if(not_paused) to all gameplay system registrations in each plugin's build() method. Do NOT pause camera, HUD, save/load, or pause toggle systems.

userStories.title: Fix tech tree — connect unlocked recipes to crafting system
userStories.description: As a player, I want blueprints and research to actually gate crafting recipes so that progression matters.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an optional tech_key: Option<&'static str> field to the Recipe struct in crafting.rs
Recipes at AdvancedForge and Ancient tiers have tech_key set to their ALL_RECIPES name (e.g. 'steel_sword', 'ancient_blade')
Hand, Workbench, Forge, and Campfire recipes have tech_key: None (always available)
handle_crafting_input reads TechTree resource and filters out recipes where tech_key is Some but not in unlocked_recipes
available_recipes() method accepts a &TechTree parameter and checks is_unlocked() for gated recipes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add tech_key field to Recipe struct. Update CraftingSystem::new() to set tech_key on the ~12 advanced recipes. Modify available_recipes() to take &TechTree and filter. Update handle_crafting_input to pass TechTree to available_recipes(). The existing TechTree.is_unlocked() method already works.

userStories.title: Fix crafting station proximity — check placed buildings not inventory
userStories.description: As a player, I want crafting to require standing near a placed workbench/forge rather than carrying it in my inventory.
acceptanceCriteria: Add CraftingStation { tier: CraftingTier } component to building.rs
When placing a Workbench, Forge, Campfire, AdvancedForge, or AncientWorkstation building, also insert CraftingStation component with appropriate tier
handle_crafting_input queries all CraftingStation entities with Transform, checks if player is within 64px of each
Builds a set of available CraftingTiers from nearby stations
Hand tier is always available (no station needed)
Remove the inventory.has_items() checks for station detection
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add CraftingStation component. In building.rs place_building system, add CraftingStation when the BuildingType corresponds to a station. In crafting.rs handle_crafting_input, add Query<(&CraftingStation, &Transform)> parameter, compute distances to player, build HashSet<CraftingTier> from nearby stations.

userStories.title: Fix dungeon room connectivity — add doorways between rooms
userStories.description: As a player, I want to walk between dungeon rooms instead of being trapped in isolated boxes.
acceptanceCriteria: Dungeon rooms have 2-tile-wide doorway openings on the sides connecting to adjacent rooms
First room has doorway on right wall, last room on left wall, middle rooms on both sides
Doorways are centered vertically on the connecting wall
A corridor of floor tiles connects adjacent rooms through the gap
Player and enemies can walk through the corridor between rooms
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In generate_dungeon, after spawning each room's walls, calculate doorway positions. Modify spawn_room_walls to skip wall tiles at the doorway location. Add spawn_corridor function that fills the gap between rooms with floor tiles. The gap_tiles=2 already exists — just need floor tiles in the gap and wall openings.

userStories.title: Fix hermit spawning — add hermits to world generation
userStories.description: As a player, I want to find hermit NPCs in the world so that I can hear lore and get rare trades.
acceptanceCriteria: spawn_hermit is called from world/mod.rs spawn_chunk_objects for appropriate biomes
Hermits spawn at ~0.2% probability per chunk in Forest, Swamp, Mountain, Desert, Tundra biomes
Hermits use the existing spawn_hermit() function from npc.rs
Hermits are tagged as ChunkObject with appropriate chunk_pos so they load/unload with chunks
At most 1 hermit per chunk
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In world/mod.rs spawn_chunk_objects, add a hermit spawn check similar to dungeon entrance. Use position_hash with a unique offset. Call npc::spawn_hermit(). Make sure spawn_hermit is pub. Add ChunkObject component to hermit entities.

userStories.title: Fix save/load — persist world seed
userStories.description: As a player, I want my world to look the same after loading a save so that chunks regenerate correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: SaveData struct includes a seed: u32 field
On save, read seed from WorldState resource and store in SaveData
On load, restore the seed to WorldState before any chunk generation
WorldState generates a random seed on startup if no save is loaded (already does this)
Loading a save produces the same world terrain as when saved
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add seed field to SaveData. In save_game, read WorldState.seed. In load_game, set WorldState.seed before clearing and regenerating chunks. The existing WorldState resource should have a pub seed field.

userStories.title: Fix save/load — persist tech tree, armor, lore, spawn point, explored chunks
userStories.description: As a player, I want all my progress to be saved — not just inventory and buildings.
acceptanceCriteria: SaveData includes tech_tree_unlocks: Vec<String> from TechTree.unlocked_recipes
SaveData includes research_points: u32 from TechTree.research_points
SaveData includes armor_helmet, armor_chest, armor_shield as Option<String> from ArmorSlots
SaveData includes lore_entries: Vec<usize> from LoreRegistry
SaveData includes spawn_point: (f32, f32) from SpawnPoint resource
SaveData includes explored_chunks: Vec<(i32, i32)> from ExploredChunks
All fields are restored on load
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extend SaveData with new fields. In save_game system, read each resource and populate fields. In load_game, restore each resource. For ArmorSlots, save item names as strings and parse back to ItemType on load. For ExploredChunks, save as Vec of tuples.

userStories.title: Add chest storage with interaction UI
userStories.description: As a player, I want to interact with placed chests to store and retrieve items.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ChestStorage component with slots: Vec<Option<InventorySlot>> of 18 slots
When placing a Chest building, also insert ChestStorage component
E key near a chest (within 32px) opens a chest UI panel
ChestUI shows chest contents as text list and player inventory below
Number keys 1-9 transfer items from player hotbar to chest
Arrow keys + Enter transfer items from chest to player inventory
Press E again or Escape to close chest UI
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add ChestStorage component in building.rs. Add ChestUI resource with is_open, target_entity. Create chest_interaction system in building.rs. Render chest UI in hud.rs. Transfer items between Inventory and ChestStorage.

userStories.title: Add gathering progress bar and visual feedback
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see a progress bar when gathering resources instead of objects just disappearing.
acceptanceCriteria: While player holds LMB on a gatherable object, a progress bar renders above the object
Progress bar is a colored rectangle that fills from left to right proportional to remaining health
Progress bar background is dark gray, fill is green
Progress bar is 24px wide and 4px tall, positioned 12px above the object
Progress bar entity is spawned when gathering starts, despawned when gathering stops or object is destroyed
Object sprite scale shrinks from 1.0 to 0.7 as health decreases
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add GatheringTarget component that tracks which entity is being gathered. Spawn progress bar as a child entity with Sprite. In gather_resources system, update the progress bar fill width and object scale each frame. Remove progress bar when player stops gathering or moves away.

userStories.title: Sprite-based player health and hunger bars
userStories.description: As a player, I want visual health and hunger bars instead of just text numbers.
acceptanceCriteria: Health bar renders as a red/green rectangle in the top-left corner of the screen
Health bar is 120px wide, 12px tall with a dark border
Green fill width is proportional to current_hp / max_hp
Hunger bar renders below health bar, same dimensions, yellow/orange fill
Both bars update each frame based on Player Health and Hunger components
Text labels 'HP' and 'FOOD' render to the left of each bar
Numeric values still show to the right of bars (e.g. '80/100')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Bevy Node/Style UI with BackgroundColor for the bars. Create bar container, background, and fill as UI nodes in spawn_hud. Update fill width in update_status_hud based on health/hunger percentages. Use Style { width: Val::Px(fill_width), .. } for the fill node.

userStories.title: Visual hotbar with slot borders and selection indicator
userStories.description: As a player, I want a visual hotbar at the bottom of the screen with bordered slots and a highlight on the selected slot.
acceptanceCriteria: 9 hotbar slots render as bordered rectangles at the bottom-center of the screen
Each slot is 40x40px with a 2px dark border
The selected slot has a bright yellow/white border instead of dark
Slot numbers 1-9 render in the top-left corner of each slot
Item names render centered in filled slots
Stack counts render in the bottom-right corner for stacked items
Empty slots show a darker background color
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create hotbar UI using Bevy's Node/Style system. Spawn 9 slot container nodes in spawn_hud positioned at bottom-center. Use BackgroundColor and BorderColor for styling. Update text content in update_hud based on inventory hotbar slots.

userStories.title: Inventory grid panel with item display
userStories.description: As a player, I want a proper inventory grid panel that shows all 36 slots when I open inventory.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab key toggles an inventory panel overlay
Panel renders as a dark semi-transparent background covering the right side of screen
36 slots displayed in a 9x4 grid layout using Bevy UI nodes
Each slot shows item name abbreviated to 3-4 characters, stack count, and durability if applicable
Slots are 36x36px with borders matching hotbar style
Empty slots show darker background
Panel has 'INVENTORY' header text
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add InventoryPanelOpen resource or field to Inventory. Toggle with Tab. Create inventory panel UI nodes in spawn_hud (initially hidden via Display::None). Update slot contents in update_hud. Use Style flexbox for grid layout. Toggle visibility with Display::Flex / Display::None.

userStories.title: Improved crafting panel with recipe ingredients display
userStories.description: As a player, I want the crafting panel to clearly show recipe ingredients, what I have, and what I'm missing.
acceptanceCriteria: Crafting panel renders as a dark overlay on the right side when C is pressed
Each recipe line shows: recipe name, required ingredients with counts
Ingredients the player HAS are colored green, ingredients MISSING are colored red
Selected recipe is highlighted with a brighter background
Panel header shows current crafting tier access (e.g. 'Stations: Hand, Workbench')
Panel scrolls if more recipes than fit on screen (max 15 visible)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Refactor the existing CraftingHudText from a single Text node into a Bevy UI panel with Node/Style. Each recipe gets its own row with ingredient text nodes. Color ingredients based on inventory.has_items() check. Use Bevy UI scrolling or just clamp the visible window.

userStories.title: Player facing direction and movement animation
userStories.description: As a player, I want my character to face the direction they're moving so the game feels responsive.
acceptanceCriteria: Player sprite flips horizontally when moving left vs right (Sprite.flip_x)
Add a PlayerFacing component with Up, Down, Left, Right variants
PlayerFacing updates each frame based on movement direction (largest axis wins)
Player sprite color shifts slightly when moving (lighter) vs idle (normal) to indicate state
Walking creates a subtle bobbing effect (±1px Y oscillation at 8Hz)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add PlayerFacing enum and component. In player_movement, after computing velocity, set facing direction. Apply Sprite.flip_x based on facing. Add a walk_bob timer that oscillates transform.translation.y by ±1px when moving.

userStories.title: Hit feedback — screen shake, enemy knockback, and flash
userStories.description: As a player, I want combat to feel impactful with screen shake and knockback.
acceptanceCriteria: When player hits an enemy, camera shakes for 0.15 seconds (±3px random offset)
Add ScreenShake resource with timer and intensity fields
Camera system applies random offset to camera transform when shake timer > 0
Hit enemies are knocked back 8px away from the player over 0.1 seconds
Add Knockback component with direction, timer, and distance fields
Knockback system moves entities with Knockback component each frame
Boss hits shake harder (±6px for 0.2 seconds)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add ScreenShake resource in camera.rs. Trigger shake in combat.rs player_attack when damage dealt. Add Knockback component, insert on hit enemies. Apply knockback in a new knockback_system. Camera system reads ScreenShake and adds random offset.

userStories.title: Death screen with stats before respawn
userStories.description: As a player, I want a death screen showing what killed me and my stats before respawning.
acceptanceCriteria: On death, a full-screen dark overlay appears for 3 seconds before respawn
Overlay shows 'YOU DIED' in large red text centered on screen
Below the title, show: 'Day X', 'Items Lost: Y', 'Enemies Killed: Z'
Add DeathStats resource tracking total_kills (incremented in combat)
After 3 seconds, respawn proceeds as normal (teleport to spawn, drop items at death marker)
Pressing any key skips the death screen timer
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add DeathScreen resource with timer and active flag. Add DeathStats resource with total_kills. In death.rs handle_death, set DeathScreen.active = true and timer = 3.0. Spawn a UI overlay. Tick timer, on expiry proceed with respawn logic. Increment DeathStats.total_kills in combat enemy death.

userStories.title: Add audio system foundation with bevy_audio
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an audio system set up so we can add sound effects and music.
acceptanceCriteria: Use Bevy's built-in AudioPlugin (already included in DefaultPlugins)
Add a GameAudio resource that tracks volume settings (master, sfx, music) as f32 0.0-1.0
Add a play_sfx helper function that spawns an AudioPlayer entity with the given audio source handle
Add an AudioAssets resource that will hold handles to loaded audio files
Add a SoundEvent event enum with variants: Hit, Gather, Build, Craft, Pickup, MenuOpen, MenuClose, Death, BossRoar
Add a handle_sound_events system that reads SoundEvent and plays placeholder audio (just logs for now since no audio files exist)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create a new audio.rs module. Add GameAudio resource, SoundEvent event, AudioAssets resource. Register the plugin. The actual audio files don't exist yet — the system just needs to be wired up so that other systems can fire SoundEvent::Hit etc. and the audio system will eventually play them. For now, log events.

userStories.title: Wire sound events into combat, gathering, and building
userStories.description: As a developer, I want combat, gathering, and building systems to fire sound events so audio plays when assets are added.
acceptanceCriteria: player_attack system sends SoundEvent::Hit when dealing damage to an enemy
Enemy death sends SoundEvent::Death (for enemy death sound)
Boss encounters send SoundEvent::BossRoar when boss first enters chase state
gather_resources sends SoundEvent::Gather on each gathering tick
place_building sends SoundEvent::Build when a building is placed
Crafting sends SoundEvent::Craft when a recipe is crafted
Item pickup sends SoundEvent::Pickup
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add EventWriter<SoundEvent> parameter to relevant systems. Fire events at appropriate points. Import SoundEvent from audio module. Do not add audio files — just wire the events.

userStories.title: Particle effects for gathering and combat
userStories.description: As a player, I want visual particle effects when chopping trees, mining rocks, and hitting enemies.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a Particle component with velocity, lifetime, color, and size fields
Add a particle_system that moves particles by velocity each frame and despawns when lifetime expires
When gathering a tree (Wood object), spawn 3-5 brown particles flying outward
When gathering a rock (Stone object), spawn 3-5 gray particles flying outward
When hitting an enemy, spawn 3-5 red particles at hit location
Particles start at 4px size and shrink to 0 over their lifetime (0.3-0.5 seconds)
Particles have random velocity spread (±30px/s in X and Y)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create particles.rs module with Particle component and ParticlePlugin. Add spawn_particles(commands, pos, color, count) helper function. Call from gather_resources and player_attack systems. Particle system runs each frame: translate by velocity * dt, shrink scale, despawn when lifetime <= 0.

userStories.title: Item pickup float animation
userStories.description: As a player, I want dropped items to float toward me when I walk near them so pickup feels satisfying.
acceptanceCriteria: Dropped items (from gathering, enemy drops) have a DroppedItem component with a 0.5s spawn delay before they can be picked up
When player is within 48px of a dropped item and spawn delay has elapsed, the item moves toward the player at 200px/s
When the item reaches within 8px of the player, it is consumed and added to inventory
Dropped items bob up and down slightly (±2px at 3Hz) while on the ground
Items spawn with a small random offset (±8px) from the source position
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add DroppedItem component with item_type, count, spawn_timer. Create spawn_dropped_item helper. In gather_resources and enemy death, spawn DroppedItem entities instead of directly adding to inventory. Add pickup_dropped_items system that attracts items toward player and adds to inventory.

userStories.title: Day/night visual atmosphere — ambient light color shifts
userStories.description: As a player, I want the world to visually change between day and night with lighting color shifts.
acceptanceCriteria: ClearColor (background) shifts based on time of day: dawn=warm orange, day=dark blue-gray, dusk=purple, night=very dark
Add AmbientLight resource that varies color and intensity by time of day
Dawn (0.2-0.3): warm golden tint, medium brightness
Day (0.3-0.7): neutral bright light
Dusk (0.7-0.8): warm orange-red tint, dimming
Night (0.8-0.2): cool blue-purple tint, dark
Transitions are smooth (lerp between phases)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In daynight.rs update system, compute a color based on time_of_day float. Set ClearColor resource and optionally adjust a global AmbientTint resource. Chunk rendering could multiply tile colors by ambient tint for full effect, but just ClearColor is the minimum viable approach.

userStories.title: Minimap border, player arrow, and POI markers
userStories.description: As a player, I want the minimap to have a border, a directional player indicator, and markers for key locations.
acceptanceCriteria: Minimap has a 2px dark border around its edges
Player indicator is a small white arrow/triangle pointing in the player's facing direction instead of a plain dot
Dungeon entrances show as small purple dots on the minimap (if in explored chunks)
Bed/spawn point shows as a small green dot on the minimap
NPC traders show as small yellow dots when visible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In minimap.rs update_minimap, after rendering terrain, draw border pixels. Draw player as an arrow shape (3 pixels in facing direction). Query DungeonEntrance, SpawnPoint, Trader entities and plot their positions on the minimap image relative to player position.

userStories.title: Enemy variety — patrol paths, alert states, and varied AI
userStories.description: As a player, I want enemies to feel more alive with varied behavior patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Patrol state moves enemies in a random direction for 2-4 seconds, then picks a new direction
Enemies have a detection_range field (default 120px, bosses 200px)
When player enters detection range, enemy transitions to Alert state (pauses 0.5s) then Chase
When player leaves chase range (detection_range + 80px), enemy returns to Patrol
Add a brief Attack state when enemy is within melee range — pause 0.3s, deal damage, return to Chase
Enemy attack has a 1.0s cooldown between hits
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extend EnemyState enum with Alert and Attack variants. Add detection_range and attack_cooldown fields to Enemy component. Refactor enemy_ai system to use the full state machine: Idle -> Patrol -> Alert -> Chase -> Attack -> Chase. Add patrol direction and timer to Enemy.

userStories.title: Improved world objects — visual variety and interaction hints
userStories.description: As a player, I want world objects (trees, rocks, etc.) to look more varied and show interaction prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: Trees have 3 color variants per biome (slight hue/brightness variation based on position hash)
Rocks have 2 size variants (small 8px, large 12px) based on position hash
When player is within gathering range (32px) of an object, a small 'E' or tool icon text appears above it
Interaction hint text is semi-transparent and only shows for the nearest valid object
Berry bushes have a distinct pink/red color tint to differentiate from trees
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In world/mod.rs spawn_chunk_objects, use position_hash to select color variants and size variants. Add InteractionHint component. Create show_interaction_hints system that finds the nearest gatherable object to the player and spawns/moves a hint text entity above it.

userStories.title: Building placement validation — prevent invalid placement
userStories.description: As a player, I want the build preview to show red when placement is invalid (on water, overlapping buildings).
acceptanceCriteria: Build preview ghost turns red when placement would be on a non-walkable tile (water, lava)
Build preview ghost turns red when overlapping an existing building entity
Right-click does NOT place the building when preview is red
Build preview is green/white when placement is valid
Check uses the same tile lookup as player collision (chunk tile data at target position)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In building.rs update_build_preview, check tile walkability at the preview position. Query existing Building entities for overlap. Set preview Sprite color to red (invalid) or semi-transparent white (valid). Guard place_building with the same validity check.

userStories.title: Save chest contents and farm plots
userStories.description: As a player, I want my chests and farms to persist across save/load.
acceptanceCriteria: SaveData includes chest_data: Vec with entity position and all 18 slot contents
On load, chest Building entities are spawned with ChestStorage populated from save data
SaveData includes farm_data: Vec with position, crop type, and growth progress
On load, FarmPlot entities are respawned with correct growth state
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add SaveChestData and SaveFarmData structs with Serialize/Deserialize. In save_game, query all ChestStorage entities and FarmPlot entities. In load_game, spawn them back. This depends on US-008 (chest storage) being completed first.

userStories.title: Smooth camera with dead zone and edge panning
userStories.description: As a player, I want the camera to follow me smoothly with a small dead zone so it doesn't feel jerky.
acceptanceCriteria: Camera has a 32px dead zone around center — camera doesn't move when player is within dead zone
When player moves outside dead zone, camera lerps toward player at 5.0 * delta_time
Camera never overshoots player position (clamped interpolation)
Camera smoothly handles the transition when entering/exiting dungeons (snap rather than lerp on teleport)
Existing zoom functionality still works
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In camera.rs camera_follow system, compute delta between camera and player. If delta magnitude < 32px (dead zone), don't move. Otherwise, lerp camera translation toward player. Detect teleports (delta > 500px) and snap instead of lerp.

userStories.title: HUD feedback messages — floating text for game events
userStories.description: As a player, I want floating text feedback when things happen (damage numbers, item pickup, crafting success).
acceptanceCriteria: Add FloatingText component with text, color, timer, and velocity fields
Floating text spawns at world position, drifts upward at 30px/s, fades out over 1.5 seconds
When player deals damage, spawn red floating text showing damage number at enemy position
When player takes damage, spawn red floating text showing damage number at player position
When player picks up an item, spawn white floating text showing '+1 ItemName' at pickup position
When crafting succeeds, spawn green floating text showing 'Crafted: ItemName'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create FloatingText component. Add floating_text_system that moves text upward, decreases alpha, and despawns when timer expires. Add spawn_floating_text helper. Call from combat, gathering/pickup, and crafting systems.

userStories.title: Biome transition blending and visual distinctness
userStories.description: As a player, I want biome boundaries to feel distinct with clear visual differences between biomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Each biome's grass/ground tiles use a distinct color palette (not just slight variations)

Forest: rich greens, Coastal: sandy yellows, Swamp: murky olive, Desert: warm tan, Tundra: icy white-blue, Volcanic: dark red-brown, Fungal: purple-teal, Crystal: pale blue-white, Mountain: gray-brown
Water tiles in different biomes have slight color variation (tropical blue in Coastal, dark in Swamp, icy in Tundra)
Biome name displays briefly (3 seconds) as floating text when entering a new biome
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Refactor tile color generation in world/generation.rs to use per-biome color palettes. Add last_biome tracking to player. When biome changes, spawn a centered floating text with biome name. Ensure each biome is visually immediately recognizable.

userStories.title: Main menu with New Game, Load Game, and Quit options
userStories.description: As a player, I want a main menu when the game starts instead of immediately spawning into the world.
acceptanceCriteria: Game starts in a MainMenu state (not gameplay)
Main menu shows game title 'DRIFTLANDS' in large text, centered
Three buttons: 'New Game', 'Continue' (only if save exists), 'Quit'
New Game generates a random seed and starts gameplay
Continue loads the existing save file
Quit exits the application
Add GameState enum with MainMenu and Playing variants as a Bevy State
All gameplay systems only run in Playing state
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add GameState as a Bevy States enum. Move all gameplay plugins to run in Playing state using .run_if(in_state(GameState::Playing)). Create MainMenuPlugin that renders title and buttons. On New Game, transition to Playing. On Continue, load save then transition. Use Bevy UI buttons with Interaction component.

userStories.title: Tutorial hints for first-time players
userStories.description: As a new player, I want contextual hints that teach me the controls and basic mechanics.
acceptanceCriteria: Add TutorialState resource tracking which hints have been shown
First hint on spawn: 'WASD to move, hold LMB near trees to gather' (shows for 8 seconds)
After first gather: 'Press C to open crafting. Try making a Workbench!' (shows for 8 seconds)
After first craft: 'Press B to enter build mode. Q to cycle, RMB to place.' (shows for 8 seconds)
After first build: 'Night is coming... craft a weapon!' (shows on first nightfall)
Hints render as semi-transparent text box at top-center of screen
Hints only show once per game (not on every trigger)
TutorialState is saved with game state so hints don't repeat
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add TutorialState resource with HashSet<String> of shown hints. Add check_tutorial_triggers system that watches for events (first gather, first craft, first build, first nightfall). Show hint via a TutorialHint UI node. Save shown_hints in SaveData.

userStories.title: Enemy spawn balancing — scale with distance and day count
userStories.description: As a player, I want enemies to get harder as I explore further and survive longer.
acceptanceCriteria: Enemy spawn count cap increases from 5 to: 5 + (day_count / 5) capped at 20
Enemies spawning further than 500px from world origin get +10% HP per 500px distance
Boss HP scales with distance: +20% per 1000px from origin
Night enemy spawn rate increases slightly each day (1% base + 0.1% per day, capped at 3%)
Add distance_from_origin field to Enemy on spawn for scaling calculations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In enemy_spawn system, calculate spawn cap from DayNightCycle.day_count. Scale HP using player position distance from origin. Apply multiplier when spawning Enemy component. For bosses, apply similar scaling in spawn_dungeon_boss using dungeon anchor distance.

userStories.title: Crafting station visual distinction — unique colors per tier
userStories.description: As a player, I want placed crafting stations to look visually distinct from regular buildings.
acceptanceCriteria: Workbench building renders as brown color (distinct from wood walls)
Forge building renders as dark red-orange color
Campfire building renders as warm orange-yellow with smaller size (12x12)
AdvancedForge renders as dark metallic gray-blue
AncientWorkstation renders as glowing purple-blue
Each station has a slight glow effect (1px brighter border sprite spawned behind it)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Update building_color() function in building.rs to return distinct colors for each station type. Add a glow child sprite behind station entities with slightly larger size and a low-alpha bright color. Spawn glow in place_building when building type is a station.

userStories.title: Keyboard shortcut reference overlay
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see all keyboard shortcuts at a glance without pausing.
acceptanceCriteria: F1 key toggles a semi-transparent controls overlay on the left side of the screen
Overlay lists all controls grouped by category: Movement, Combat, Building, Crafting, Inventory, Other
Each entry shows key and action (e.g. 'WASD - Move', 'LMB - Attack/Gather')
Overlay does not pause the game (just informational)
Overlay dismisses when F1 is pressed again or Escape is pressed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add ControlsOverlay resource with is_visible: bool. Toggle with F1. Render as a Bevy UI panel with semi-transparent background. List all keybindings as Text nodes. Add .run_if check so it hides when crafting/trade menus are open.

userStories.title: World object density and variety per biome
userStories.description: As a player, I want each biome to feel unique with different object densities and exclusive resources.
acceptanceCriteria: Forest has highest tree density (8-12 per chunk), mushrooms, and berry bushes
Desert has cacti, sandstone rocks, and rare oasis with water tiles
Tundra has sparse trees, ice formations, and frozen ore deposits
Volcanic has obsidian nodes, sulfur vents (yellow sprites), and no trees
Fungal has giant mushrooms (larger sprites), glowing spore objects, and bio-luminescent gel nodes
Crystal caves have crystal clusters, gemstone nodes, and echo stone objects
Coastal has driftwood, shell deposits, and seaweed patches near water
Each biome-exclusive object drops its unique resources when gathered
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Refactor spawn_chunk_objects biome match to have more varied and biome-specific object types. Add new WorldObjectType variants as needed (SulfurVent, GiantMushroom, ShellDeposit, etc). Adjust spawn probabilities per biome. Add drop tables for new object types.

userStories.title: Dungeon loot variety — chests and item drops
userStories.description: As a player, I want dungeons to have loot chests in rooms and better item drops.
acceptanceCriteria: Each non-boss dungeon room has a 60% chance to contain a loot chest entity
Loot chests are interactable (E key) and drop 2-4 random items from a dungeon loot table
Dungeon loot table includes: IronOre, CrystalShard, HealthPotion, Arrow (x5), Blueprint (rare 10%), JournalPage (rare 15%)
Loot chest despawns after being opened (one-time)
Loot chest is a colored sprite (yellow/gold, 10x10px) distinct from enemies and walls
Cave spiders have a 30% chance to drop a random item on death (CaveSlime, SpiderSilk, or Berry)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add DungeonChest component with opened: bool. Spawn in generate_dungeon for non-boss rooms. Add chest_interaction_dungeon system. Add dungeon_loot_table() function. Add CaveSlime and SpiderSilk ItemType variants for spider drops.

userStories.title: Weather gameplay effects — rain heals farms, storms damage
userStories.description: As a player, I want weather to have gameplay effects beyond visual particles.
acceptanceCriteria: Rain: farm plots grow 25% faster during rain (multiply growth speed)
Rain: fire-based crafting stations (Campfire, Forge) cannot be used outdoors during rain (only if no roof above)
Storm: player takes 1 damage every 15 seconds if not under a roof
Storm: enemies move 20% slower during storms
Snow: movement speed reduced by 15% for player and enemies in Tundra/Mountain during snow
Clear: no effects (baseline)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In weather.rs, add gameplay effect systems. Check WeatherSystem current weather type. For roof detection, check if there's a Roof building within 32px of the entity. Apply speed modifiers via a WeatherModifier component or direct speed multiplication in movement systems.

userStories.title: Season visual effects — color palette shifts
userStories.description: As a player, I want the world to visually change with the seasons.
acceptanceCriteria: Spring: grass tiles are bright green, flowers spawn as small colored dots
Summer: grass tiles are deeper green, warm color tint on ClearColor
Autumn: grass tiles shift to yellow-orange, trees shift to orange-red-brown
Winter: grass tiles shift to white-gray, water edges get lighter (ice), trees are bare (brown only)
Season transition applies gradually over the first day of each season (color lerp)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add SeasonColorModifier resource with season-dependent color multipliers for grass, tree, and water tiles. In season.rs update, compute the modifier. Pass modifier to chunk rendering (world/mod.rs) when generating chunk images. Requires re-rendering nearby chunk images when season changes.

userStories.title: Food buff system — cooked food gives temporary stat boosts
userStories.description: As a player, I want eating cooked food to give temporary buffs beyond just hunger restoration.
acceptanceCriteria: CookedBerry: restores 25 hunger + 10% speed boost for 60 seconds
CookedMeat: restores 40 hunger + 15% damage boost for 60 seconds
CookedFish: restores 30 hunger + health regeneration (2 HP/10s) for 90 seconds
BerryPie (new recipe: CookedBerry x3 at Campfire): restores 60 hunger + all three buffs for 45 seconds
Active food buff shows in the status HUD with remaining time
Only one food buff active at a time (new food replaces old buff)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extend the existing ActiveBuff / BuffType system to include food buffs. Add FoodBuff component or extend BuffType with Regen variant. In the eat system (wherever food consumption happens — likely inventory.rs use_item), apply the appropriate buff. Add BerryPie ItemType and recipe.

userStories.title: Minimap toggle and fullscreen map view
userStories.description: As a player, I want to toggle the minimap off and open a larger map view with M key.
acceptanceCriteria: M key toggles a fullscreen map overlay (similar size to inventory panel)
Fullscreen map renders the same explored terrain at 3x the minimap resolution
Fullscreen map shows the same POI markers as minimap (dungeons, spawn, NPCs)
Tab to close fullscreen map (or M again)
N key toggles the corner minimap on/off for players who want more screen space
Fullscreen map has a dark semi-transparent border
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add MapViewOpen resource. M toggles. When open, render a larger Image (360x360) using the same explored chunk data. Position as a centered UI node. N toggles minimap_visible flag to show/hide the corner minimap.
